title: Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize
summary: RL Nabors argues that most applications default to frontier models unnecessarily, incurring latency, energy, and cost penalties when smaller local models would suffice. The talk covers evaluation vocabulary and frameworks for deciding when to deploy local versus frontier models, using production traces and LLM-as-judge techniques to measure model performance objectively.
writeup: RL Nabors of Arize presents a decision framework for determining when small local models can replace frontier models in production. The talk covers evaluation vocabulary including golden datasets and LLM-as-judge, methods for building evals from production traces, and concrete cases such as agentic harnesses and in-browser moderation. The central argument: most frontier model calls carry unnecessary latency, energy, and cost penalties in situations where smaller local alternatives are sufficient.
